---
title: "Offset Headings"
subtitle: "Quarto Extension"
author:
  - name: "Mickaël CANOUIL, _Ph.D._"
    orcid: "0000-0002-3396-4549"
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
format:
  html:
    output-file: index
    embed-resources: true
  typst:
    output-file: example-typst
    papersize: a4
    margin:
      x: 2.5cm
      y: 2.5cm
format-links:
  - html
  - typst
filters:
  - offset-headings
---

## Untouched Heading

This heading has no attribute and keeps its original level.

## Shifted Heading {offset-headings-by="1"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-3 heading; the surrounding headings keep their original levels.

## Pulled-up Heading {offset-headings-by="-1"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-1 heading.

## Recursive Cascade {offset-headings-by="1"}

Cascading is on by default, so this heading and its nested headings all shift by 1.

### Nested Heading

This level-3 heading becomes a level-4 heading.

#### Deeper Heading

This level-4 heading becomes a level-5 heading.

## Back to Normal

This heading at the original base level stops the cascade and stays at level 2.

## Non-recursive Shift {offset-headings-by="1" offset-headings-recursive="false"}

This level-2 heading becomes a level-3 heading, but its nested heading is left untouched.

### Untouched Nested Heading

This level-3 heading keeps its original level.
